chapter_title.en: Chapter on Qasamah (oath of Allegation in Cases of murder)

text: 5177 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سَهْلٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَشْيَاخِهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "إِنَّ أَمِيرَ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ سُئِلَ عَنْ رَجُلٍ كَانَ جَالِساً مَعَ قَوْمٍ فَمَاتَ وَ هُوَ مَعَهُمْ أَوْ رَجُلٍ وُجِدَ فِي قَبِيلَةٍ أَوْ عَلَى دَارِ قَوْمٍ فَادُّعِيَ عَلَيْهِمْ قَالَ "لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِمْ قَوَدٌ وَ لاَ يُطَلُّ دَمُهُ عَلَيْهِمُ اَلدِّيَةُ" ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5177 - Muhammad ibn Sahl narrated from his father, from some of his elders, from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as), who said:
"The Commander of the Faithful (as) was asked about a man who was sitting with a group of people and died while among them, or a man who was found dead in a tribe or near the house of some people, and they were accused of his death.
Imam (as) said: 'There is no retaliation (qisas) upon them, nor is his blood wasted. However, the blood money (diyyah) is upon them.'"
